name: explore_project
description: Quickly explore a project's structure: find source files (Python, Go, C, Rust, TypeScript), list top-level directories, count files by type, identify config files, and show key subdirectories. Auto-detects language based on file counts. Automatically excludes build artifact directories (target/, build/, dist/, node_modules/, .git/, .venv/) from all searches. Supports targeting a specific subdirectory with --subdir.
when_to_use: Use at the very beginning of a task to understand the project layout. Replaces multiple 'find', 'ls', and 'wc' calls. Supports Python, Go, C, Rust, and TypeScript projects out of the box. For Rust projects, shows Cargo.toml/lock info, workspace members, and checks common workspace subdirectories (core/, cli/, engine/, parser/, ast/). Use --subdir <path> to explore a specific subdirectory (e.g., src/schema/) when you need a focused view of a particular module.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Primary language: 'py' (Python), 'go' (Go), 'c' (C), 'rs' (Rust), 'ts' (TypeScript), or 'auto' (detect from project, default: auto)

parameters.name: max_files
parameters.description: Maximum number of source filenames to show (default: 50)

parameters.name: --subdir
parameters.description: Target a specific subdirectory within the project (e.g., src/schema). Shows directory structure, file counts, and source files limited to that subdirectory. Useful when you need to focus on a specific module rather than the whole project.

examples.expected: Project structure overview with detected language, source files (excluding build artifacts), directory listing, file counts, config files, and key subdirectory listing.
Project overview treating /app as a TypeScript project, showing up to 30 .ts/.tsx source files.
Project overview treating /app as a Rust project, showing .rs files (excluding target/), Cargo config with workspace members, and src/ directory.
Focused exploration of the src/schema subdirectory in a TypeScript project, showing up to 50 .ts/.tsx source files within that directory.
Explores the src/ directory with auto-detected language, showing up to 100 source files.

cost_saving_rationale: Combines 5-10 separate bash commands (find, ls, wc, multiple greps for config) into a single script call, cutting round trips and reducing output volume by only showing essential info in one pass. Excluding build artifact directories prevents token waste from showing compiled/generated files. The --subdir flag replaces the common pattern of running 'find <subdir> -type f -name "*.ts"' to focus on a specific module, saving an additional tool call.
